sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.103 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Let$H$be a proper subgroup of a group$G$and let elements$a,b H$have distinct prime orders~$p, q$. Suppose that, for every$g G H$, the subgroup$a,b^g$is a finite Frobenius group with complement of order~$pq$. Does the subgroup generated by the union of the kernels of all Frobenius subgroups of$G$with complement$a$intersect$a$trivially? The case where all groups$a,b^g$,$g G$, are finite is of specia...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
